name: 花的结构与传粉
subject: biology
curriculum_points: 概念4 植物有自己的生命周期，可以制造有机物，直接或间接地为其他生物提供食物，参与生物圈的水循环，并维持碳氧平衡。
4.2.1 花是植物的生殖器官，其结构有利于完成传粉和受精。
4.2.2 植物可以通过不同的媒介（如风、昆虫）完成传粉。

resource_references.text: # 13.生物学 > 来源: 教育部《义务教育课程标准（2022年版）》 义务教育 生物学课程标准 (2022 年版 ) 作用，必须坚持马克思主义的指导地位，体现马克思主义中国化最新 成果，体现中国和中华民族风格，体现党和国家对教育的基本要求， 体现国家和民族基本价值观，体现人类文化知识积累和创新成果。 义务教育课程规定了教育目标、教育内容和教学基本要求，体现 国家意志，在立德树人中发挥着关键作用。2001 年颁布的《义务教 育课程设置实验方案》和 2011 年颁布的义务教育各课程标准，坚持 了正确的改革方向，体现了先进的教育理念，为基础教育质量提高作 出了积极贡献。随着义务教育全面普及，教育需求从“有学上”转向 “上好学”，必须进一步明确“培养什么人、怎样培养人、为谁培养 人”，优化学校育人蓝图。当今世界科技进步日新月异，网络新媒体 迅速普及，人们生活、学习、工作方式不断改变，儿童青少年成长环 境深刻变化，人才培养面临新挑战。义务教育课程必须与时俱进，进 行修订完善 以习近平新时代中国特色社会主义思想为指导，全面贯彻党的教 育方针，遵循教育教学规律，落实立德树人根本任务，发展素质教

supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 花的结构与传粉

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 生物学
- **学段**: middle
- **年级**: 7
- **章节/领域**: 植物的结构与生理
- **内容来源**: TeachAny 课标知识点卫星文件

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
> 概念4 植物有自己的生命周期，可以制造有机物，直接或间接地为其他生物提供食物，参与生物圈的水循环，并维持碳氧平衡。
> 4.2.1 花是植物的生殖器官，其结构有利于完成传粉和受精。
> 4.2.2 植物可以通过不同的媒介（如风、昆虫）完成传粉。

## 二、教学目标

- 理解「花的结构与传粉」的核心概念、方法与应用场景。
- 能结合材料、问题或真实情境解释「花的结构与传粉」相关现象。
- 能用规范的学科语言表达思路、依据和结论。

## 三、教学重难点

- 教学重点：建立对「花的结构与传粉」核心概念和基本方法的准确理解。
- 教学难点：把知识迁移到新的问题、材料或真实情境中。

## 五、教学建议

### 5.1 教学方法
- 以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「花的结构与传粉」教学。
- 通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
- 围绕课标要点“概念4 植物有自己的生命周期，可以制造有机物，直接或间接地为其他生物提供食物，参与生物圈的水循环，并维持碳氧平衡。”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

### 5.2 学习活动设计
1. 以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「花的结构与传粉」教学。
2. 通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
3. 围绕课标要点“概念4 植物有自己的生命周期，可以制造有机物，直接或间接地为其他生物提供食物，参与生物圈的水循环，并维持碳氧平衡。”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

## 七、课件制作参考

- 以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「花的结构与传粉」教学。
- 通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
- 围绕课标要点“概念4 植物有自己的生命周期，可以制造有机物，直接或间接地为其他生物提供食物，参与生物圈的水循环，并维持碳氧平衡。”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

## 八、相关知识扩展

- 联系健康、生态、农业或生物技术案例，讨论「花的结构与传粉」与现实生活的关系。
- 通过模型图、显微图或调查资料拓展学生对生命系统的理解。
supplements.textbook_summary:
## 教材化讲解参考（由课标要点生成）

### 核心概念
花的结构与传粉是生物学学习中的重要知识点。教学时应围绕课标要点“概念4 植物有自己的生命周期，可以制造有机物，直接或间接地为其他生物提供食物，参与生物圈的水循环，并维持碳氧平衡。”展开，先明确概念边界，再通过例子、图示、实验、语篇或案例帮助学生建立可迁移的理解。

### 学习路径
1. 情境导入：以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「花的结构与传粉」教学。
2. 探究建构：通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
3. 应用迁移：围绕课标要点“概念4 植物有自己的生命周期，可以制造有机物，直接或间接地为其他生物提供食物，参与生物圈的水循环，并维持碳氧平衡。”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

### 例题设计方向
可设计一道“识别核心概念”的基础题，一道“解释现象或文本材料”的理解题，一道“迁移到真实情境”的应用题，并要求学生说明依据。

deep_textbook_snippets.text: By the end of this section, you will be able to do the following: • Explain why angiosperms are the dominant form of plant life in most terrestrial ecosystems • Describe the main parts of a flower and their functions • Detail the life cycle of a typical gymnosperm and angiosperm • Discuss the similarities and differences between the two main groups of flowering plants From their humble and still obscure beginning during the early Jurassic period, the angiosperms—or flowering plants—have evolved to dominate most terrestrial ecosystems (Figure 26.13). With more than 300,000 species, the angiosperm phylum (Anthophyta) is second only to insects in terms of diversification. FIGURE 26.13 Flowers. These flowers grow in a botanical garden border in Bellevue, WA. Flowering plants dominate terrestrial landscapes. The vivid colors of flowers and enticing fragrance of flowers are adaptations to pollination by animals like insects, birds, and bats. (credit: Myriam Feldman) The success of angiosperms is due to two novel reproductive structures: flowers and fruits. The function of the flower is to ensure pollination, often by arthropods, as well as to protect a developing embryo. The colors and patterns on flowers offer specific signals to many pollinating insects or birds and bats that have coevolved with them. For example, some patterns are visible only in the ultraviolet range of light, which can be seen by arthropod pollinators. For some pollinators, flowers advertise themselves as a reliable source of nectar. Flower scent also helps to select its pollinators. Sweet scents tend to att

> **[Figure 762, Page 960]** A large illustration or diagram (544×425px) related to: Pollination, Birds, Many, species, small, birds. This image appears on page 960 of the textbook.
![Figure 762 - A large illustration or diagram (544×425px) related to: Poll](OpenStax_Biology_2e_images/img_0762_57bbb5c5.jpg)
FIGURE 32.15 A person knocks pollen from a pine tree. FIGURE 32.16 These male (a) and female (b) catkins are from the goat willow tree (Salix caprea). Note how both structures are light and feathery to better disperse and catch the wind-blown pollen. Pollination by Water Some weeds, such as Australian sea grass and pond weeds, are pollinated by water. The pollen floats on water, and when it comes into contact with the flower, it is deposited inside the flower.
Pollination by Deception Orchids are highly valued flowers, with many rare varieties (Figure 32.17). They grow in a range of specific habitats, mainly in the tropics of Asia, South America, and Central America. At least 25,000 species of orchids have been identified. 32.2 • Pollination and Fertilization
> **[Figure 763, Page 961]** A large illustration or diagram (544×714px) related to: FIGURE, person, knocks, pollen, pine, tree. This image appears on page 961 of the textbook.
![Figure 763 - A large illustration or diagram (544×714px) related to: FIGU](OpenStax_Biology_2e_images/img_0763_57bbb5c5.jpg)
> **[Figure 764, Page 961]** A wide diagram or timeline (1117×387px) related to: FIGURE, person, knocks, pollen, pine, tree. This image appears on page 961 of the textbook.
![Figure 764 - A wide diagram or timeline (1

Explore this website (https://openstax.org/l/pollinators2) for additional information on pollinators. More than 80 percent of angiosperms depend on animals for pollination (technically the transfer of pollen from the anther to the stigma). Consequently, plants have developed many adaptations to attract pollinators. With over 200,000 different plants dependent on animal pollination, the plant needs to advertise to its pollinators with some specificity. The specificity of specialized plant structures that target animals can be very surprising. It is possible, for example, to determine the general type of pollinators favored by a plant by observing the flower’s physical characteristics. Many bird or insect-pollinated flowers secrete nectar, which is a sugary liquid. They also produce both fertile pollen, for reproduction, and sterile pollen rich in nutrients for birds and insects. Many butterflies and bees can detect ultraviolet light, and flowers that attract these pollinators usually display a pattern of ultraviolet reflectance that helps them quickly locate the flower's center. In this manner, pollinating insects collect nectar while at the same time are dusted with pollen (Figure 26.21). Large, red flowers with little smell and a long funnel shape are preferred by hummingbirds, who have good color perception, a poor sense of smell, and need a strong perch. White flowers that open at night attract moths. Other animals—such as bats, lemurs, and lizards—can also act as pollinating agents. Any disruption to these interactions, such as the disappearance of bees, for example as

> **[Figure 758, Page 956]** A large illustration or diagram (900×772px) related to: FIGURE, series, micrographs, shows, male, female. This image appears on page 956 of the textbook.
![Figure 758 - A large illustration or diagram (900×772px) related to: FIGU](OpenStax_Biology_2e_images/img_0758_220c10d0.png)
common. FIGURE 32.11 (a) Angiosperms are flowering plants, and include grasses, herbs, shrubs and most deciduous trees, while (b) gymnosperms are conifers. Both produce seeds but have different reproductive strategies. (credit a: modification of work by Wendy Cutler; credit b: modification of work by Lews Castle UHI)
View an animation of the double fertilization process of angiosperms. Access multimedia content (https://openstax.org/books/biology-2e/pages/32-1-reproductive-development-andstructure) 32.2 Pollination and Fertilization

deep_worked_example.stem: 根据教材片段，设计一道关于「花的结构与传粉」的现象解释或实验探究题。
deep_worked_example.solution_outline: 先描述现象或实验条件，再指出关键概念和证据，最后给出解释或结论。
deep_worked_example.evidence_excerpt: By the end of this section, you will be able to do the following: • Explain why angiosperms are the dominant form of plant life in most terrestrial ecosystems • Describe the main parts of a flower and their functions • Detail the life cycle of a typical gymnos

errors.description: 把「花的结构与传粉」当作孤立事实记忆，不能说明结构、功能和生命过程之间的关系。
解释生命现象时缺少证据，容易把相关关系误认为因果关系。

exercises.stem: 用结构与功能或过程与结果的关系解释「花的结构与传粉」。
exercises.answer: 应扣住课标要点：概念4 植物有自己的生命周期，可以制造有机物，直接或间接地为其他生物提供食物，参与生物圈的水循环，并维持碳氧平衡。，说明生命现象、结构/过程和功能意义。

exercises.stem: 举一个生活、健康或生态案例，说明「花的结构与传粉」的应用价值。
exercises.answer: 需写出案例、涉及的生物学原理以及对生活或环境的意义。

real_world: 观察人体健康、植物生长、生态环境或食品生产中与「花的结构与传粉」相关的生命现象。
